name: mario-pipeline-tools

metadata.name: mario-pipeline-tools
metadata.summary: Base classes and helpers for common data pipeline tasks

documentation: A set of base classes and helpers for common data pipeline tasks, whether using in local control scripts or in Airflow tasks.

functions.name: mario.metadata.metadata_from_json
documentation: Factory method for creating a Metadata instance from a JSON file

functions.name: mario.metadata.metadata_from_manifest
documentation: Factory method for creating a Metadata instance from a JSON file in manifest format

functions.name: mario.metadata.metadata_from_excel
documentation: Factory method for creating a Metadata instance from an Excel file

methods.name: mario.metadata.Metadata.get_hierarchies
documentation: Returns a list of all hierarchies in the metadata

methods.name: mario.metadata.Metadata.get_hierarchy
documentation: Returns an ordered list of the item names in the specified hierarchy

methods.name: mario.dataset_builder.DatasetBuilder.validate_metadata
documentation: Checks whether the metadata matches the specification

methods.name: mario.dataset_builder.DatasetBuilder.remove_redundant_hierarchies
documentation:
Modifies the metadata to remove any hierarchies that contain
only one item.
\:return: None

functions.name: mario.excel_builder.rotate_list
documentation:
Rotates a list n positions. Used to push measure to the front of the data
\:param list_1: the list to rotate
\:param n: number of positions to rotate
\:return: rotated list

classes.name: mario.excel_builder.ExcelBuilder

methods.name: mario.excel_builder.ExcelBuilder.create_workbook
documentation:
Creates a write-only workbook and builds
content in streaming mode to conserve memory

methods.name: mario.excel_builder.ExcelBuilder.__format_cell__
documentation:
Copy attributes from src to dst. Attributes are shallow-copied to avoid
TypeError: unhashable type: 'StyleProxy'

methods.name: mario.excel_builder.ExcelBuilder.__replace_template_values
documentation: Replaces template values in a cell with metadata from the order

methods.name: mario.excel_builder.ExcelBuilder.create_notes_only
documentation: Create a Notes page only, to accompany CSV outputs

methods.name: mario.excel_builder.ExcelBuilder.__update_notes__
documentation: Updates the notes page

methods.name: mario.excel_builder.ExcelBuilder.__range__
documentation:
Determine the cell range based on the dataset size
\:return: a cell range e.g "A1:C26"
Class for creating Excel outputs

classes.name: mario.query_builder.QueryBuilder
documentation:
Generic interface for a query builder. Subclass this to create your own custom
query builders to pass to a DataExtractor in a Configuration.

classes.name: mario.query_builder.ViewBasedQueryBuilder
documentation: Creates a very simple 'get everything from the view' query

classes.name: mario.query_builder.SubsetQueryBuilder

methods.name: mario.query_builder.SubsetQueryBuilder.create_totals_query
documentation:
Constructs a 'total only' query by combining the selections and constraints.
\:return: an array containing the query prepared statement, and the parameters
TODO make this more generic

methods.name: mario.query_builder.SubsetQueryBuilder.create_query
documentation:
Constructs the query by combining the selections and constraints.
\:return: an array containing the query prepared statement, and the parameters

methods.name: mario.query_builder.SubsetQueryBuilder.create_constraints
documentation:
Builds a set of constraints from the query; this consists of both
defined constraints, and the implicit constraint on years. Uses the
'in' method for determining constraint syntax
\:param q: the Query object
\:return: the Query object with constraints
Class for building SQL queries as selections from a view with aggregation

classes.name: mario.validation.DataTypes
documentation:
Base data types, that are mapped onto specific
types used in Pandas, Hyper, SQL etc

classes.name: mario.validation.Validator

methods.name: mario.validation.Validator.__get_column_name__
documentation: Returns the column name for a metadata item

methods.name: mario.validation.Validator.__get_column_data_type__
documentation: Returns the type of a metadata item from the data

methods.name: mario.validation.Validator.__get_column_values__
documentation: Returns an array of the unique values in the data for an item

methods.name: mario.validation.Validator.__get_minimum_maximum_values__
documentation: Returns the minimum value of an item from the data

methods.name: mario.validation.Validator.__contains_nulls__
documentation: Returns True if the item has any Null values in the data

methods.name: mario.validation.Validator.__get_data_for_hierarchy__
documentation: Returns the dataframe for a hierarchy

methods.name: mario.validation.Validator.__get_column_with_segmentation__
documentation: Returns the data series for an item grouped by the segmentation column specified

methods.name: mario.validation.Validator.__check_hierarchy__
documentation: Checks whether the named hierarchy conforms to a tree structure

methods.name: mario.validation.Validator.__check_for_anomalies__
documentation:
Checks a series for anomalies by looking for absolute z-scores above threshold
\:param series: the series to check
\:param threshold: the threshold level
\:return: True if an anomaly is present

methods.name: mario.validation.Validator.check_hierarchies
documentation:
Checks the validity of hierarchies, i.e. that they form a tree structure and
adds any anomalies found to errors/warnings

methods.name: mario.validation.Validator.check_category_anomalies
documentation:
Checks to see if we get anomalies in how categories are split e.g. over time
and adds them to warnings
\:param segmentation: the field to segment by
\:return: None

methods.name: mario.validation.Validator.check_data_type
documentation: Checks whether the item has the correct data type

methods.name: mario.validation.Validator.check_column_present
documentation: Checks whether the column for an item is present in the data

methods.name: mario.validation.Validator.check_nulls
documentation: Checks if there are any null values for an item

methods.name: mario.validation.Validator.check_domain
documentation: Checks that the values for an item conform to the domain in its specification

methods.name: mario.validation.Validator.check_range
documentation: Checks whether the values of an item fall within expected range

methods.name: mario.validation.Validator.check_pattern_match
documentation: Checks that the values for an item match the pattern in the specification

methods.name: mario.validation.Validator.check_quality_checks
documentation: Checks whether an item has any quality rules used in validation

methods.name: mario.validation.Validator.validate_data
documentation:
Performs validation of data
\:param allow_nulls: If True, nulls are shown as warnings rather than errors
\:param check_hierarchies: If True, perform hierarchy consistency check
\:param detect_anomalies: If True, perform anomaly detection
\:param segmentation: Optional - field name to use for anomaly detection
\:return: True if no validation errors are found

Base class for validators. Extended for specific data implementations
such as Pandas Dataframe, Tableau HyperFile etc.

classes.name: mario.validation.DataFrameValidator
documentation:
A validator for Pandas Dataframes, or data extractors that
use a dataframe as their internal representation.

classes.name: mario.validation.HyperValidator
documentation: A validator for hyper files

functions.name: mario.dataset_specification.get_year_starts
documentation:
Converts the array of academic year strings for a request into an array of year start integers
\:return: an array of integers for each year start

classes.name: mario.dataset_specification.DatasetSpecification
documentation:
Base class/interface for dataset specifications. Use an implementation specific to the
format of specification used.

classes.name: mario.data_extractor.Configuration
documentation:
Configuration for a data extractor. This can include:
* A connection string: an ODBC connection string for SQL extraction
* A view name: A view as the base for the SQL code (H+, TDSA only)
* A hook: An object or function used to access data, e.g. S3
* A file path: A data file to load as the source of data, e.g. data.csv
* A QueryBuilder class: instantiate and use to build a SQL query

methods.name: mario.data_extractor.DataExtractor.__get_column_name__
documentation: Returns the column name for a metadata item

methods.name: mario.data_extractor.DataExtractor.__minimise_data__
documentation: Minimise data so we only keep the columns in the spec

methods.name: mario.data_extractor.DataExtractor.save_query
documentation:
Output the query used
\:param file_path:
\:return:

classes.name: mario.data_extractor.HyperFile
documentation:
Wrapper for a HyperFile as a data extractor - use when no data needs to be extracted,
and we just want to treat a hyper as a hyper with no conversion to/from dataframe

classes.name: mario.data_extractor.StreamingDataExtractor

methods.name: mario.data_extractor.StreamingDataExtractor.get_total
documentation:
For totals when streaming data we need to run a totals SQL query separate
from the main query and use the results of this
\:return: the total value of the query

methods.name: mario.data_extractor.StreamingDataExtractor.stream_sql_to_hyper
documentation:
Write From SQL to .hyper using streaming. No data is held in memory
apart from chunks of rows as they are read.
Optionally, data can be validated as it is read.

methods.name: mario.data_extractor.StreamingDataExtractor.stream_sql_to_csv
documentation:
Write From SQL to CSV using streaming. No data is held in memory
apart from chunks of rows as they are read.
Optionally, data can be validated as it is read.

methods.name: mario.data_extractor.StreamingDataExtractor.stream_sql_to_csv_using_bcp
documentation:
Builds table on server, then extracts it to CSV using the bcp program. No data is held in
memory. Data cannot be validated without reading it in from the generated CSV.
TODO Note that the generated SQL must start with 'SELECT * FROM' - make this more generic
TODO Provide an option to not drop/create table if it already exists.

Extension to basic data extractor with additional functions
supporting streaming data from SQL to output formats without
holding any data in memory using a data frame

classes.name: mario.data_extractor.DataFrameExtractor
documentation: Wrapper for a DataExtractor using an existing DataFrame
